_comment: MOCKUP/DESIGN data. Quotes taken from two public CERT-In primaries (see source links), re-verified 2026-06-12. This is the NATIONAL UMBRELLA view: CERT-In literally names 'CBOM' and 'automated cloud-native cryptographic discovery' — CryptaMap's exact category. HONESTY: these are advisory/recommended (whitepaper + technical guidelines), NOT an enforced sectoral mandate; that tier is disclosed.
title: CERT-In — Quantum Cyber Readiness & the CBOM/QBOM (national framing)
summary: CERT-In (India's national CERT) is the umbrella over the sector regulators. Its quantum-readiness whitepaper and its SBOM/QBOM/CBOM technical guidelines name a 'Cryptographic Bill of Materials' and 'automated cloud-native cryptographic discovery' as the foundation of PQC migration — i.e. they describe CryptaMap's category by name. These are advisory/recommended documents (not an enforced rule), which is stated up front; their value is as the authoritative national description of what a tool like CryptaMap should produce.

documentsHeld.title: Transitioning to Quantum Cyber Readiness (CERT-In white paper, with SISA)
Technical Guidelines on SBOM, QBOM & CBOM (v2.0)

claims.headline: CERT-In names the 'Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM)' as the inventory foundation
claims.quote: … an inventory … often referred to as a Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM), of the components …
claims.where: CIWP-2025-0002, Transitioning to Quantum Cyber Readiness
claims.cryptaMapNote: CryptaMap produces exactly this — a CycloneDX CBOM of the organisation's (AWS) cryptographic assets. CryptaMap IS the artifact CERT-In names as the foundation of quantum readiness.

claims.headline: CERT-In's CBOM definition matches CryptaMap's output fields
claims.quote: … an inventory of cryptographic assets—including algorithms, keys, protocols, certificates, and dependencies—capturing critical metadata such as usage patterns and expiration dates.
claims.where: CERT-In Technical Guidelines on SBOM/QBOM/CBOM v2.0
claims.cryptaMapNote: CryptaMap's CBOM captures algorithms, keys, protocols and certificates with metadata (key size, TLS version, cert validity, rotation) for the AWS surface — a near-1:1 match to CERT-In's defined CBOM elements. Honest gap: 'dependencies' (software component graph) is a full-SBOM concern CryptaMap does not cover.

claims.headline: CERT-In calls for automated, cloud-native cryptographic discovery
claims.quote: Cloud-native discovery for containerized and serverless environments …
claims.cryptaMapNote: CryptaMap is automated, cloud-native cryptographic discovery for AWS (containers, serverless, managed services) — the exact tooling category CERT-In recommends over manual inventory.

honestCaveats: These are CERT-In ADVISORY documents (a whitepaper + recommended technical guidelines), NOT an enforced sectoral regulation. They describe best practice and define the CBOM/QBOM; they do not, by themselves, impose a binding obligation the way a sectoral circular does.

CERT-In is the national umbrella; the binding obligations sit with the sector regulators (SEBI / IRDAI here). Use this view as the authoritative national description, and the regulator pages for what is actually required of you.
CryptaMap covers the AWS crypto surface and is evidence, not certification.
